briefdescription.para: Expect a test to fail.
Expect a test to pass.
The expected result of a test.
The result of the current running test.

detaileddescription.para: It's possible that the setup function sets the result to ZTEST_RESULT_SUITE_* which will apply the failure/skip to every test in the suite.

briefdescription.para: Each enum member represents a distinct phase of execution for the test binary.

detaileddescription.para: TEST_PHASE_FRAMEWORK is active when internal ztest code is executing; the rest refer to corresponding phases of user test code.

briefdescription.para: Setup function to run before running this suite.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the data structure that will be used throughout this test suite

briefdescription.para: Function to run before each test in this suite.

parameterdescription.para: The test suite's fixture returned from setup()

briefdescription.para: Function to run after each test in this suite.

parameterdescription.para: The test suite's fixture returned from setup()

briefdescription.para: Teardown function to run after running this suite.

parameterdescription.para: The test suite's data returned from setup()

briefdescription.para: An optional predicate function to determine if the test should run.

detaileddescription.para: If NULL, then the test will only run once on the first attempt.

parameterdescription.para: The current state of the test application.

simplesect.para: True if the suite should be run; false to skip.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* ztest_rule_cb) (const struct ztest_unit_test *test, void *data)
memberdef.name: ztest_rule_cb

briefdescription.para: Test rule callback function signature.

detaileddescription.para: The function signature that can be used to register a test rule's before/after callback. This provides access to the test and the fixture data (if provided).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the unit test in context
Pointer to the test's fixture data (may be NULL)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *state, bool shuffle, int suite_iter, int case_iter)
memberdef.name: ztest_run_all

briefdescription.para: Default entry point for running or listing registered unit tests.

parameterdescription.para: The current state of the machine as it relates to the test executable.
Shuffle tests
Test suite repetitions.
Test case repetitions.

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *state, bool shuffle, int suite_iter, int case_iter)
memberdef.name: ztest_run_test_suites

briefdescription.para: Run the registered unit tests which return true from their predicate function.

parameterdescription.para: The current state of the machine as it relates to the test executable.
Shuffle tests
Test suite repetitions.
Test case repetitions.

simplesect.para: The number of tests that ran.

briefdescription.para: Fails the test if any of the registered tests did not run.

detaileddescription.para: When registering test suites, a pragma function can be provided to determine WHEN the test should run. It is possible that a test suite could be registered but the pragma always prevents it from running. In cases where a test should make sure that ALL suites ran at least once, this function may be called at the end of test_main(). It will cause the test to fail if any suite was registered but never ran.

briefdescription.para: Fail the currently running test.

detaileddescription.para: This is the function called from failed assertions and the like. You probably don't need to call it yourself.

briefdescription.para: Pass the currently running test.

detaileddescription.para: Normally a test passes just by returning without an assertion failure. However, if the success case for your test involves a fatal fault, you can call this function from k_sys_fatal_error_handler to indicate that the test passed before aborting the thread.

briefdescription.para: Skip the current test.
A 'before' function to use in test suites that just need to start 1cpu.

parameterdescription.para: The test suite's data

briefdescription.para: A 'after' function to use in test suites that just need to stop 1cpu.

parameterdescription.para: The test suite's data

briefdescription.para: Expect a test to fail (mark it passing if it failed)
Expect a test to skip (mark it passing if it failed)
Number of registered unit tests.
Number of registered test suites.

memberdef.name: ZTEST_SUITE
memberdef.initializer: struct ztest_suite_stats UTIL_CAT(z_ztest_suite_node_stats_, SUITE_NAME); \ static const STRUCT_SECTION_ITERABLE(ztest_suite_node, \ UTIL_CAT(z_ztest_test_node_, SUITE_NAME)) = { \ .name = STRINGIFY(SUITE_NAME), \ .setup = (setup_fn), \ .before = (before_fn), \ .after = (after_fn), \ .teardown = (teardown_fn), \ .predicate = PREDICATE, \ .stats = &UTIL_CAT(z_ztest_suite_node_stats_, SUITE_NAME), \ }

briefdescription.para: Create and register a ztest suite.

detaileddescription.para: Using this macro creates a new test suite. It then creates a struct ztest_suite_node in a specific linker section.
Tests can then be run by calling ztest_run_test_suites(const void *state) by passing in the current state. See the documentation for ztest_run_test_suites for more info.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the suite
A function to test against the state and determine if the test should run.
The setup function to call before running this test suite
The function to call before each unit test in this suite
The function to call after each unit test in this suite
The function to call after running all the tests in this suite

briefdescription.para: Make data section used by Ztest userspace accessible.
Make bss section used by Ztest userspace accessible.
Ztest data section for accessing data from userspace.
Create and register a new unit test.

detaileddescription.para: Calling this macro will create a new unit test and attach it to the declared suite. The suite does not need to be defined in the same compilation unit.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the test suite to attach this test
The test function to call.

briefdescription.para: Define a test function that should run as a user thread.

detaileddescription.para: This macro behaves exactly the same as ZTEST, but calls the test function in user space if CONFIG_USERSPACE was enabled.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the test suite to attach this test
The test function to call.

briefdescription.para: Define a test function.

detaileddescription.para: This macro behaves exactly the same as ZTEST(), but the function takes an argument for the fixture of type struct suite##_fixture* named fixture.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the test suite to attach this test
The test function to call.

briefdescription.para: Define a test function that should run as a user thread.

detaileddescription.para: If CONFIG_USERSPACE is not enabled, this is functionally identical to ZTEST_F(). The test function takes a single fixture argument of type struct suite##_fixture* named fixture.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the test suite to attach this test
The test function to call.

briefdescription.para: Define a test rule that will run before/after each unit test.

detaileddescription.para: Functions defined here will run before/after each unit test for every test suite. Along with the callback, the test functions are provided a pointer to the test being run, and the data. This provides a mechanism for tests to perform custom operations depending on the specific test or the data (for example logging may use the test's name).

Ordering:
Test rule's before function will run before the suite's before function. This is done to allow the test suite's customization to take precedence over the rule which is applied to all suites.
Test rule's after function is not guaranteed to run in any particular order.

parameterdescription.para: The name for the test rule (must be unique within the compilation unit)
The callback function (ztest_rule_cb) to call before each test (may be NULL)
The callback function (ztest_rule_cb) to call after each test (may be NULL)

briefdescription.para: Run the specified test suite.

parameterdescription.para: Test suite to run.
Shuffle tests
Test suite repetitions.
Test case repetitions.
Test parameter

briefdescription.para: This module eases the testing process by providing helpful macros and other testing structures.
